title: iberis saxatilis subsp. cinerea
intro: Iberis saxatilis subsp. cinerea, also known as the rock candytuft, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mustard family. It is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of isothiocyanates, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antibacterial effects, providing various health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Iberis saxatilis subsp. cinerea can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues and other health conditions.
